items.q: Do all arteries carry oxygenated blood?
items.correct: No — the pulmonary artery carries deoxygenated blood from the heart to the lungs.
items.misconception: All arteries carry oxygenated blood and all veins carry deoxygenated blood.
items.random_wrong: Arteries and veins both carry the same mixed blood through the body.
items.why: The simplification 'arteries = oxygenated, veins = deoxygenated' is taught early and not nuanced for the pulmonary circuit.

items.q: How are signals transmitted across a synapse between two neurones?
items.correct: Via neurotransmitter chemicals released from the presynaptic neurone, which bind receptors on the postsynaptic neurone.
items.misconception: The electrical signal jumps directly from one neurone to the next across the synaptic gap.
items.random_wrong: Neurones touch each other at synapses and pass current through direct contact.
items.why: Students think of nerves as 'wires' carrying electricity straight through, missing the chemical step at each synapse.

items.q: Why is a reflex action faster than a conscious response?
items.correct: The signal does not need to be processed by the brain — it travels through a simpler reflex arc via the spinal cord.
items.misconception: The brain decides reflexes faster than other actions because it skips thinking.
items.random_wrong: Reflexes are faster because they use specially fast neurones not used elsewhere.
items.why: Students assume the brain is always in charge; they don't realise reflexes specifically bypass the brain to save time.

items.q: Where is the body's natural pacemaker located?
items.why: It's Right atrium (SAN). “Left ventricle” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Why do veins have valves but arteries do NOT?
items.correct: Veins carry blood at LOW pressure so backflow is a risk
items.misconception: Veins are thicker so need them
items.why: Correct answer: Veins carry blood at LOW pressure so backflow is a risk. “Veins are thicker so need them” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: A healthy resting heart rate is about 70 beats per minute. How many beats is that in 1 hour (60 minutes)?
items.why: Exact answer: 4200. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
